objective: Use the existing native OpenClaw UI as the primary commercial conversation experience. Keep Agent Harness and ORIS behind it as tool, policy, lifecycle and evidence layers, and keep Codex as the code executor. Retire the custom ORIS chat shell from the primary user path instead of rebuilding standard conversation features.

architecture_decision.agent_harness_role: backend policy, routing, structured tool contract and fallback; not the default end-user shell
architecture_decision.oris_role: task identity, lifecycle, queue, lease, cancellation, retry, evidence and audit
architecture_decision.codex_role: implementation, test, repair, commit and controlled push
architecture_decision.custom_oris_chat_shell_role: remove from root; retain temporarily only for diagnostics or rollback under a non-default route

diagnosis.current_root_ui: custom ORIS Web Console v5 developed in the ORIS repository
diagnosis.prompt_problem: custom intent router and deterministic command parser altered normal conversational semantics and caused requests to depend on ORIS-specific rules
diagnosis.decision: do not continue rebuilding these standard UI features in the custom shell

implemented: service, storage and features response contract
pytest coverage for status code and fields
completed_real_task.acceptance_gap: README API list was requested but was not changed in the product commit
completed_real_task.final_acceptance: partial; must repair README and verify evidence before calling the task fully compliant

migration_requirements: discover the currently active native OpenClaw UI and session/history behavior without reinstalling or upgrading
archive the current custom ORIS chat sessions before removing them from the default route
switch the effective Nginx root route from Web Console v5 to the existing OpenClaw Gateway UI on port 18789
preserve WebSocket and OpenClaw token/device-pairing behavior
expose ORIS actions to OpenClaw through a stable tool/plugin contract rather than keyword parsing
retain a rollback route for the custom shell during migration
verify native new-conversation, history, switching and clear/archive behavior in the browser
verify one natural-language development task without ORIS-specific prompt syntax
repair the missing README update from the completed /capabilities task and verify product/evidence SHA
next_action: Perform a read-only discovery of the effective native OpenClaw UI route, configuration, history/session support, authentication and WebSocket contract. Then prepare a reversible Nginx/service switch that makes native OpenClaw the root UI, moves the custom ORIS shell to a rollback/diagnostic route, and does not submit another product task until browser acceptance passes.
blocked_reason: default public UI is a custom shell missing standard conversation lifecycle features and using non-native prompt routing
